title: Create database migration for life_events table
description: Create SQL migration file to add life_events table with person_id foreign key, event_type, event_date, is_recurring flag, and notes fields
acceptance_criteria: Migration file creates life_events table with all required columns
Foreign key constraint references people(id) with CASCADE delete
is_recurring defaults to false
created_at timestamp defaults to NOW()

title: Create database migration for nudges table
description: Create SQL migration file to add nudges table with person_id and life_event_id foreign keys, message, trigger_at, status, sent_at, dismissed_at fields
acceptance_criteria: Migration file creates nudges table with all required columns
Foreign key constraints reference people(id) and life_events(id) with SET NULL on delete
Status field has CHECK constraint for 'pending', 'sent', 'dismissed' values
Status defaults to 'pending'
created_at timestamp defaults to NOW()

title: Update people table schema to match architecture
description: Create migration to add missing created_at and updated_at columns to people table and update interactions table to match architecture schema
acceptance_criteria: People table has created_at and updated_at columns with NOT NULL and DEFAULT NOW()
Interactions table has interacted_at column instead of created_at for interaction timestamp
Interactions table retains created_at for record creation timestamp
All existing data is preserved during migration

title: Implement log_interaction function in people module
description: Implement log_interaction function in people.ts to create interaction records and update last_interaction_at on person
acceptance_criteria: log_interaction function accepts person name and interaction notes
Function finds person using fuzzy matching
Creates new interaction record with interacted_at timestamp
Updates person.last_interaction_at to current timestamp

title: Add tests for log_interaction function
description: Write comprehensive tests for log_interaction function covering success and error cases
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify log_interaction returns success response with interaction details
Tests verify person.last_interaction_at is updated correctly
Tests handle person not found gracefully
Tests cover fuzzy matching behavior

title: Implement create_life_event function
description: Implement create_life_event function in new tools/life_events.ts module to handle event creation with automatic recurring flag for birthdays/anniversaries
acceptance_criteria: create_life_event accepts person name, event_type, event_date, and optional notes
Birthdays and anniversaries automatically set is_recurring to true
Function uses fuzzy person name matching
Returns JSON response with created event details

title: Implement get_upcoming_life_events function
description: Implement get_upcoming_life_events function to query and return life events within a date range with recurring event adjustment
acceptance_criteria: get_upcoming_life_events accepts date range and returns events within window
Recurring events are adjusted to current year in results
Function handles errors gracefully
Returns JSON response with event list

title: Add tests for life events module
description: Write comprehensive tests for create_life_event and get_upcoming_life_events functions
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify create_life_event sets is_recurring correctly for birthdays and anniversaries
Tests verify get_upcoming_life_events returns correct date range
Tests verify recurring event date adjustment logic
Tests cover fuzzy matching and error cases

title: Implement create_nudge and dismiss_nudge functions
description: Implement create_nudge and dismiss_nudge functions in new tools/nudges.ts module
acceptance_criteria: create_nudge accepts person_id, life_event_id, message, and trigger_at timestamp
dismiss_nudge accepts nudge_id and sets status to 'dismissed' with dismissed_at timestamp
Both functions return JSON responses
create_nudge validates required fields and returns error for missing data

title: Add tests for nudges module
description: Write comprehensive tests for create_nudge and dismiss_nudge functions
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify create_nudge creates records with correct fields
Tests verify dismiss_nudge updates status and timestamp correctly
Tests verify validation of required fields
Tests handle non-existent nudge IDs gracefully

title: Add life events tool definitions to agent
description: Update agent.ts to include tool definitions for create_life_event and get_upcoming_life_events
acceptance_criteria: Life events tool definitions added: create_life_event, get_upcoming_life_events
Tool definitions include proper parameter descriptions
Tool name sets updated to include new tools

title: Add nudges tool definitions and routing to agent
description: Update agent.ts to include tool definitions for nudges and route tool calls to nudges module
acceptance_criteria: Nudges tool definitions added: create_nudge, dismiss_nudge
Tool routing added to executeTool function for nudges module
Agent can successfully call all nudge tools through the tool loop
Tests verify tool routing works correctly

title: Add log_interaction tool definition to agent
description: Update agent.ts to include log_interaction tool definition and ensure it routes to people module
acceptance_criteria: log_interaction tool definition added to peopleToolDefinitions array
Tool accepts name and notes parameters with name required
Tool is included in PEOPLE_TOOL_NAMES set for routing
executePeopleTool function handles log_interaction operation

title: Update get_person to include life events
description: Modify get_person function in people.ts to join with life_events table and include upcoming life events in the response
acceptance_criteria: get_person response includes life_events array with upcoming events
Life events show event_type, event_date, is_recurring, and notes
Recurring events are adjusted to show next occurrence date
Query efficiently joins tables using person_id

title: Add tests for get_person with life events
description: Write tests for updated get_person function to verify life events are included correctly
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify get_person includes life_events array
Tests verify response maintains backward compatibility
Tests verify recurring event date adjustment in response
Tests verify query efficiency with proper joins

title: Create scheduler module with nudge evaluator
description: Create scheduler.ts module with node-cron job that runs every 15 minutes to check for due nudges
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler exports startScheduler function to initialize cron jobs
Nudge evaluator runs every 15 minutes using node-cron
Evaluator queries nudges table for pending nudges past trigger_at
Maximum 3 nudges sent per hour to prevent spam

title: Add scheduler tests and logging
description: Write tests for scheduler module and add comprehensive logging for nudge processing
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify nudge evaluator runs on schedule
Tests verify rate limiting (max 3 nudges per hour)
Scheduler logs all nudge processing activities
Tests verify pending nudges are correctly identified

title: Add automatic nudge creation to create_life_event
description: Update create_life_event to automatically create nudges 7 days before birthdays and 14 days before anniversaries
acceptance_criteria: Birthday events automatically create nudge 7 days before event_date
Anniversary events automatically create nudge 14 days before event_date
Nudge message includes person name and event type
Nudge creation handles date calculations correctly for recurring events

title: Add tests for automatic nudge creation
description: Write tests for automatic nudge creation logic in create_life_event
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify nudges are created with correct trigger dates
Tests verify nudge messages are formatted correctly
Tests verify function continues if nudge creation fails
Tests verify date calculations for recurring events

title: Implement nudge sending in scheduler
description: Update scheduler to send nudge messages via Telegram bot when nudges are due
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler sends nudge messages to TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_ID
Each nudge message includes a Dismiss button with callback data
Nudge status updated to 'sent' with sent_at timestamp after successful send
Dismiss button callback includes nudge ID for later dismissal

title: Add error handling and tests for nudge sending
description: Add error handling for failed nudge sends and write comprehensive tests
acceptance_criteria: Failed sends are logged but don't prevent other nudges from processing
Tests verify nudge status is updated correctly after send
Tests verify Telegram message format with Dismiss button
Tests verify error handling for failed sends

title: Implement dismiss nudge callback parsing in bot
description: Update bot callback handling to parse nudge dismiss actions and extract nudge ID
acceptance_criteria: Bot parses dismiss callback data to extract nudge ID
Callback data format is consistent with scheduler's Dismiss button
Bot handles malformed callback data gracefully
Callback query is answered to remove loading state

title: Implement dismiss nudge API call and UI update in bot
description: Update bot to call orchestrator dismiss_nudge endpoint and remove inline keyboard from message
acceptance_criteria: Bot calls orchestrator POST /dismiss-nudge endpoint with nudge ID
Successful dismissal removes the inline keyboard from the message
Bot handles dismiss callback errors gracefully
Tests verify API call and UI update behavior

title: Add dismiss-nudge endpoint to orchestrator
description: Create POST /dismiss-nudge endpoint in orchestrator to handle nudge dismissal requests from bot
acceptance_criteria: POST /dismiss-nudge endpoint accepts nudge_id in request body
Endpoint calls dismiss_nudge tool function
Returns success/error response to bot
Validates nudge_id is provided and is valid number
Endpoint follows same error handling patterns as other orchestrator endpoints

title: Initialize scheduler in orchestrator startup
description: Update orchestrator index.ts to start the scheduler when the service starts up
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator calls startScheduler() during service initialization
Scheduler starts before HTTP server begins accepting requests
Startup logs indicate scheduler has been initialized
Service continues to start even if scheduler initialization fails (with error logging)

title: Update shared types for Phase 3 data structures
description: Add TypeScript interfaces for life events, nudges, and callback actions to shared types
acceptance_criteria: LifeEvent interface with id, person_id, event_type, event_date, is_recurring, notes, created_at
Nudge interface with id, person_id, life_event_id, message, trigger_at, status, sent_at, dismissed_at, created_at
CallbackAction type updated to include dismiss action with nudgeId
All interfaces match database schema column names and types
